sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.4g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.4g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 6.6g. glycemic index, sugar, and starch are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 6.6g. glycemic index, sugar, and starch are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 2.8g. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 2.8g. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 85% DV, vitamin c at 99% DV, vitamin a at 12% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin k at 85% DV, vitamin c at 99% DV, vitamin a at 12% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. very strong micronutrient payoff because likely to challenge spinach for elite vegetable status. good fibre support because solid maintenance-and-repair fit. strong cruciferous reputation because viewed as a flagship healthy vegetable.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. taste can be polarising because not universally loved. prep matters a lot because overcooking hurts it badly. can be rough on digestion for some because a practical downside.

displayItems.title: can be rough on digestion for some

closing.summary: Broccoli has a genuinely strong overall case, especially very strong micronutrient payoff and good fibre support. the downsides are there, but taste can be polarising and prep matters a lot do not outweigh the upside. best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
